title: Device · core (status filter, SNOMED type, manufacture/expiration dates, UDI/lot)
notes:
Branch dimension: how Device__device_exposure (mapspec/etl/Device__device_exposure.sql + mapspec/views/Device__device_exposure.view.json) emits one device_exposure row per Device, and which Devices are dropped.

Column derivation (verified against the live cdm_ours_fhir.device_exposure row for fhir.device a884ee77-5b13-7783-ceb8-2a1aee76b940):
  device_exposure_id              = referenceToId(Device.id)
  person_id                       = referenceToId(Device.patient.getReferenceKey())
  device_concept_id               = std.concept_id  (SNOMED type code --Maps to--> standard Device-domain concept)
  device_exposure_start_date      = Device.manufactureDate::date
  device_exposure_start_datetime  = Device.manufactureDate::timestamp  (WALL-CLOCK; the FHIR +00:00 offset is dropped by ::timestamp)
  device_exposure_end_date        = Device.expirationDate::date
  device_exposure_end_datetime    = Device.expirationDate::timestamp
  device_type_concept_id          = 32817  (constant)
  unique_device_id                = left(Device.udiCarrier.first().deviceIdentifier, 255)
  production_id                   = left(Device.lotNumber, 255)
  device_source_value             = left(SNOMED type code, 50)
  device_source_concept_id        = src.concept_id  (the SNOMED concept itself)
  quantity, provider_id, visit_occurrence_id, visit_detail_id, unit_concept_id, unit_source_value, unit_source_concept_id  = NULL (always)

STATUS FILTER (the load-bearing branch): the stage-2 SQL ends with `WHERE v.status_code IN ('active','inactive')`. So:
  - status='active'   -> emitted
  - status='inactive' -> emitted (still a real exposure)
  - status='entered-in-error' / 'unknown' / anything else -> NO row (Device dropped). Asserted via "omop": {} (device_exposure produced-but-empty).

SNOMED-MAPS-TO REQUIREMENT: the SELECT INNER-JOINs vocab.concept (SNOMED, by concept_code) -> concept_relationship 'Maps to' (invalid_reason IS NULL) -> standard Device-domain concept. A Device whose type.coding lacks a SNOMED system code, or whose code has no standard Device mapping, produces no row. All three Synthea device codes used here map to THEMSELVES as standard Device concepts (170615005=4015740 'Home nebulizer', 337414009=4224372 'Glucometer', 228869008=4045112 'Manual wheelchair'), so device_concept_id == device_source_concept_id for each.

VALUES IN THE ACTIVE VARIANT ARE COPIED VERBATIM from the live pipeline (cdm_ours_fhir.device_exposure for device a884ee77-...): device_concept_id 4015740, start 1989-10-29 20:26:17, end 2014-11-13 20:26:17, unique_device_id 43856429819831, production_id 1106. status='inactive' and the filtered status are SYNTHETIC (Synthea only emits status='active'), constructed to exercise the WHERE branch; their concept/date values are still verified against vocab.concept and the SQL casts.

Each Device is self-contained — no Patient/Encounter resource is required (person_id is just referenceToId of the patient reference key, no FK lookup into another t_cdm table), so every variant isolates cleanly.

cases.desc: status=active, SNOMED type + manufactureDate + expirationDate + UDI + lotNumber -> full device_exposure row (values copied from live cdm_ours_fhir for device a884ee77-...)
status=inactive is STILL emitted (WHERE status_code IN ('active','inactive')); SNOMED 337414009 Glucometer, manufacture/expiration dates only, no UDI/lot -> unique_device_id & production_id NULL
status=entered-in-error is FILTERED OUT (not in ('active','inactive')) -> zero device_exposure rows, even though the SNOMED 228869008 Manual wheelchair maps cleanly

status=active, SNOMED 228869008 Manual wheelchair, manufactureDate present but NO expirationDate -> device_exposure_end_date & device_exposure_end_datetime are NULL (OMOP end is nullable; expiration_date::date of an absent field is NULL). Start still comes from manufactureDate; device maps to itself as standard Device 4045112

status=active but the SNOMED type code has NO standard Device mapping -> INNER JOIN to standard Device-domain concept fails -> zero device_exposure rows (the SNOMED-Maps-To requirement, independent of the status filter)

type.text: Type 2 diabetes mellitus (not a device — Condition-domain SNOMED)
